name: GT-taylor-calculus
source: Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:503-512 (Banach-algebra Taylor function calculus). VERBATIM: "Let $\calB$ be a Banach algebra and $f$ a function of one complex variable represented by its Taylor series $f(x)=\sum_{n=0}^{\infty}a_n(x-x_0)^n$ with the convergence radius $\ge r$. Then $f(X)$ is well-defined, commutes with $X$, and satisfies a certain bound for all elements $X\in\calB$ sufficiently close to $x_0I$:" ... "f(X)=a_0I+\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n(X-x_0I)^n\quad\:" ... "\|f(X)-f(x_0I)\|\le \sum_{n=1}^{\infty}|a_n|\ts\|X-x_0I\|^n." INSTANTIATION: this gives, for f(x)=x^{-1/2} expanded about x0=1 (radius >= 1) and X=S^2 with ||S^2-1*I||<1, that the partial sums g_N(S^2)=a_0 I + sum_{n=1}^{N} a_n (S^2-I)^n CONVERGE (the series f(X) is well-defined = the partial sums converge in the Banach algebra norm, i.e. OPERATOR norm on End(.)) to R=(S^2)^{-1/2}, AND the tail bound ||g_N(X)-f(X)|| <= sum_{n>N}|a_n| ||X-x0 I||^n -> 0 since sum|a_n| ||X-x0 I||^n < infinity (|.|<1). This is the op-norm convergence used in the limit-passage node N1.4. Applied in EACH algebra: A (for S_F^2) and B (for S_Phi^2), each a unital Banach algebra by hypothesis.
